title: View Transitions, Anchor, 3D Text, interpolate-size & light-dark()
description: CSS View Transitions morphing, Anchor Positioning tooltips, 3D extruded text, height:auto transitions, and adaptive light-dark() theming.

drops.title: CSS View Transitions
drops.description: Shared-element morphing between gallery cards and fullscreen scenes via document.startViewTransition().
drops.concept: CSS View Transitions API captures snapshots of the old and new DOM states and animates between them using ::view-transition-old and ::view-transition-new pseudo-elements. When the same view-transition-name appears in both states, the browser automatically morphs the element's position, size, and style — enabling native-app-quality transitions in pure web. This demo shows a 6-card scene gallery with 4 transition styles (morph, zoom, slide, crossfade) and keyboard navigation.
drops.source: Chrome View Transitions API docs + Jake Archibald demos

interaction.mouse: Click any scene card to expand it fullscreen
interaction.click: Click backdrop or Back button to collapse

drops.title: CSS Anchor Positioning
drops.description: Tooltips and detail panels that anchor to DOM elements across stacking contexts — zero JavaScript positioning.
drops.concept: CSS Anchor Positioning (Chrome 125+, Firefox 128+) adds anchor-name to designate any element as a named anchor, and position-anchor + anchor() to position floats relative to it. The key win: it works across stacking contexts and scroll containers — something that always required JS. @position-try provides fallback strategies to keep panels in the viewport. This demo shows anchored tooltips on a nav and a detail panel that follows list item selection.
drops.source: CSS Anchor Positioning W3C spec + Chrome explainer

interaction.mouse: Hover buttons to see anchored tooltips; click list items to open detail panel

drops.title: 3D Extruded Text
drops.description: Stacked text-shadow layers create isometric depth, with @property animating the hue continuously.
drops.concept: Stacking many small text-shadow offsets (1px, 2px, 3px … Npx in the same direction) creates the illusion of a 3D extrusion. By registering a CSS custom property for the hue via @property (enabling it to be animated), the shadow color cycles smoothly. Four variants are shown: animated hue extrusion, isometric gold, retro neon glow, and hollow stroke extrusion. The DEPTH word is also draggable to reorient the light angle.
drops.source: CSS text-shadow stacking technique — widespread in the creative CSS community

interaction.mouse: Drag the DEPTH word to change shadow direction
interaction.click: Sliders in top-right panel adjust depth, hue, speed

drops.title: interpolate-size: allow-keywords
drops.description: One CSS rule on :root unlocks height:auto transitions — no scrollHeight JavaScript needed ever again.
drops.concept: interpolate-size: allow-keywords (Chrome 129+, Firefox 130+) enables CSS to animate between concrete pixel values and intrinsic size keywords like auto, min-content, and max-content. Previously, animating to height:auto required measuring scrollHeight in JavaScript and explicitly setting a pixel value, then clearing it after the transition. Now a single :root declaration makes the browser handle interpolation. Demo shows an accordion, expanding cards, and a before/after code comparison.

interaction.click: Expand cards by clicking; speed slider in controls panel

drops.title: light-dark() Adaptive Theming
drops.description: The light-dark() CSS function writes both light and dark color values inline — no @media queries, no JS class toggling.
drops.concept: The CSS light-dark() function (Chrome 123+, Firefox 120+, Safari 17.5+) takes two color arguments and returns the first in light mode, the second in dark mode — based on the element's computed color-scheme. Combined with color-scheme: light dark on :root, every color in your design system becomes mode-aware with zero duplication. The demo shows a live palette, a component preview card, and the minimal code needed to wire it up. Toggle between modes with the pill switch.
drops.source: MDN light-dark() reference + web.dev color-scheme guide

interaction.mouse: Toggle the pill switch to swap light/dark mode
